name: The Bank of Magnolia Company
description_short: Family-owned community bank operating since 1899 in Ohio, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards across three branch locations.
description_long: The Bank of Magnolia Company was founded in 1899 by the three sons of William H. Greer at 122 North Main Street in Magnolia, Ohio. Originally named William H. Greer's Sons, the bank was formally incorporated as The Bank of Magnolia Company in 1910. Over the past century, it has grown from a single location into a regional community bank serving the Stark, Tuscarawas, and Carroll county areas. The bank remains housed in its original 1899 building, which has been expanded with additional facilities and parking. The Bank of Magnolia offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services. For personal customers, they provide checking and savings accounts, personal loans, investments, online and mobile banking, personal credit cards, debit/ATM cards, and bill pay services. For businesses, they offer commercial checking accounts, business credit cards, and solutions tailored to small, medium, and large corporations. Additional services include wire transfers, cashier's checks, mobile deposits, Zelle person-to-person payments, check printing (both in-house and deluxe online ordering), night deposits, and cash withdrawals at foreign ATMs. What distinguishes The Bank of Magnolia is its deep local roots and family-owned structure. The bank has operated continuously from the same 1899 building for over 120 years, providing stability and community connection. They offer the rare service of in-house check printing with 8-hour turnaround, available for both personal and business checks. The bank is regulated by both the State of Ohio and the FDIC, ensuring consumer protections. Their branch network includes locations in Magnolia, East Sparta, and Canton, with consistent lobby and drive-thru hours across weekdays and limited Saturday service. The Bank of Magnolia is best suited for individuals and businesses seeking traditional banking services from an established, locally-rooted institution. As a community bank, they may offer more personalized service than national chains, though specific product terms, rates, and competitive positioning are not detailed on their website. Consumers should directly contact their branches for detailed information on loan rates, deposit rates, credit card terms, and specific product features before making banking decisions.
services: Debit/ATM cards with instant issue option
In-house check printing (8-hour turnaround, starting at $12.00)
pros: In-house check printing with 8-hour turnaround available at East Sparta branch
FDIC-regulated and State of Ohio-regulated for consumer deposit protection
Family-owned bank with 125+ years of continuous operation from original 1899 building
Mobile banking, mobile deposits, and bill pay available for account management
Three convenient branch locations (Magnolia, East Sparta, Canton) with extended drive-thru hours
Offers both personal and business credit cards
Zelle person-to-person payments integrated for fast transfers
Saturday lobby hours (9am-12pm) and drive-thru service (8:30am-12pm) for weekend access
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Ohio counties (Stark, Tuscarawas, Carroll)
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, APRs, or competitive product terms
No mention of online account opening or digital-first banking features typical of modern banks
Small branch network may require travel for some customers compared to national banks
Deposit rates page exists but actual rates are not displayed on the website
best_for: Residents and business owners in Stark, Tuscarawas, and Carroll county areas seeking community banking
Small to medium-sized businesses needing commercial checking and credit solutions
Customers who value personal service and long-term banking relationships with a stable institution
Individuals who prefer traditional banking with in-person branch access and physical check printing options
diagnosis: The Bank of Magnolia is a traditional community bank best suited for individuals and businesses in its three-county Ohio service area who value local banking relationships and personalized service. Primary caveat: The website provides limited specifics on rates, fees, loan terms, or how their products compare competitively—direct contact with branches is necessary for detailed product information before choosing this bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
